To address this, we introduce the Map-guided Masking Realism Image Dif- fusion Codec (MRIDC) , designed to optimize the trade- This CVPR paper is the Open Access version, provided by the Computer Vision Foundation. Experimental results show that MRIDC achieves state-of-the-art perceptual compression quality at extremely low bitrates, effectively preserving feature consistency in key regions and advancing the rate-distortion-perception performance curve, establishing new benchmarks in bal- ancing compression efﬁciency with visual ﬁdelity.

min D, s.t. R ≤ Rc. (1)

ingly urgent. Traditional codecs and V AE-based methods
struggle to achieve such low bitrates (e.g., 0.01 bpp) with-
out incurring severe distortion.

Perception (RDP) theory [

limitation_expression: However , current methods often neglect the non-uniform complex- ity of images, limiting their ability to balance global per- ceptual quality with local texture consistency and to al- locate coding resources efﬁciently.

(1) However, with the exponential growth of data volume and the emergence of extremely bandwidth-constrained applica- tions, such as satellite communications [ 8, 9], the demand for extreme-low bitrate compression has become increas- ingly urgent.
Especially at extreme-low bitrates, the adaptive allocation of limited bitrate resources (R) by generative compression is expected to obtain better DP frontiers.

The MLM module adaptively masks la- tent representations based on the prior RoI information and allocates limited bit resources to key areas, thereby main- taining the consistency of details of the corresponding area in the reconstructed image and the original image.
method_expression: To address this, we introduce the Map-guided Masking Realism Image Dif- fusion Codec (MRIDC) , designed to optimize the trade- This CVPR paper is the Open Access version, provided by the Computer Vision Foundation.

On the encoding side, we propose a Map-guided La- tent Masking (MLM) module, which selectively masks ele- ments in the latent space based on prior information, allow- ing adaptive resource allocation aligned with image com- plexity.
With this in mind, we propose the Map-guided-Masking Realism Image Diffusion Codec (MRIDC) to achieve endogenous-adaptive perceptual image compression at extreme-low bitrate.
In our framework, images are ﬁrst en- coded into codebook indices through latent coding, where the predeﬁned vector quantization space ensures a control- lable extreme-low bitrate.
On the decoding side, we propose the Bidirectional Prediction Controllable Generation (BPCG) module, which recon- structs the masked indices by leveraging information from neighboring elements.

Conclusion Considering the potential conﬂict between extremely low bitrate compression and consistency of decoded image details, we propose an adaptive compression framework MRIDC based on the concept of RoI, which performs gen- erative compression based on vector quantization space.
experiment_expression: Experimental results demonstrate that MRIDC achieves state-of-the-art perceptual compression quality at extremely low bitrates, effectively preserving feature consistency in key regions while allocating latent representation resources 18052 in a training-free manner.

notable_sentences: However , current methods often neglect the non-uniform complex- ity of images, limiting their ability to balance global per- ceptual quality with local texture consistency and to al- locate coding resources efﬁciently.
On the decoding side, we propose the Bidirectional Prediction Controllable Generation (BPCG) module, which recon- structs the masked indices by leveraging information from neighboring elements.

On the encoding side, we propose a Map-guided La- tent Masking (MLM) module, which selectively masks ele- ments in the latent space based on prior information, allow- ing adaptive resource allocation aligned with image com- plexity.
With this in mind, we propose the Map-guided-Masking Realism Image Diffusion Codec (MRIDC) to achieve endogenous-adaptive perceptual image compression at extreme-low bitrate.

(1) However, with the exponential growth of data volume and the emergence of extremely bandwidth-constrained applica- tions, such as satellite communications [ 8, 9], the demand for extreme-low bitrate compression has become increas- ingly urgent.
18051 off between local distortion and global perceptual quality in extreme-low bitrate compression.
Abstract Leveraging the generative power of diffusion models, gen- erative image compression has achieved impressive per- ∗ Corresponding Author.
At the decoder, we introduce a BPCG module for controllable generation, based on mask predic- tion, to produce outputs with optimal perceptual quality.

By exploit- ing the rich image modeling priors inherent in generative models, these approaches allow for the reconstruction of images with minimal artifacts using extremely few code- words [ 19, 27, 28].

By leveraging the MLM and BPCG modules, MRIDC enables conditional compression based on various priors, ensuring higher consistency in re- gions of interest (RoI) while delivering exceptional percep- tual quality, as illustrated in Figure 3.

By observing the correlation be- tween the importance of regions in the original image and the redundancy of elements in the latent space, we intro- duce the Map-guided Latent Masking (MLM) module on the coding side.

Conclusion Considering the potential conﬂict between extremely low bitrate compression and consistency of decoded image details, we propose an adaptive compression framework MRIDC based on the concept of RoI, which performs gen- erative compression based on vector quantization space.

raw_sections.abstract:
Abstract
Leveraging the generative power of diffusion models, gen-
erative image compression has achieved impressive per-
∗ Corresponding Author. This work was supported by the National
Natural Science Foundation of China (62476011), and the Alibaba Inno-
vative Research Program.
ceptual ﬁdelity even at extremely low bitrates. However ,
current methods often neglect the non-uniform complex-
ity of images, limiting their ability to balance global per-
ceptual quality with local texture consistency and to al-
locate coding resources efﬁciently. To address this, we
introduce the Map-guided Masking Realism Image Dif-
fusion Codec (MRIDC) , designed to optimize the trade-
This CVPR paper is the Open Access version, provided by the Computer Vision Foundation.
Except for this watermark, it is identical to the accepted version;
the final published version of the proceedings is available on IEEE Xplore.
18051

off between local distortion and global perceptual quality
in extreme-low bitrate compression. MRIDC integrates a
vector-quantized image encoder with a diffusion-based de-
coder . On the encoding side, we propose a Map-guided La-
tent Masking (MLM) module, which selectively masks ele-
ments in the latent space based on prior information, allow-
ing adaptive resource allocation aligned with image com-
plexity. On the decoding side, masked latents are completed
using the Bidirectional Prediction Controllable Genera-
tion (BPCG) module, which guides the constrained genera-
tion process within the diffusion model to reconstruct the
image. Experimental results show that MRIDC achieves
state-of-the-art perceptual compression quality at extremely
low bitrates, effectively preserving feature consistency in
key regions and advancing the rate-distortion-perception
performance curve, establishing new benchmarks in bal-
ancing compression efﬁciency with visual ﬁdelity. Our
code can be found at
https://github.com/xjc97/
mridc.
raw_sections.introduction:
1. Introduction
With advances in image capture and generation technolo-
gies, the volume of digital image content has surged, un-
derscoring the need for efﬁcient image compression tech-
niques. Traditional codecs [
1–3] and V ariational Autoen-
coder (V AE)-based end-to-end compression approaches [4–
7] minimize compression Distortion ( D) under Rate con-
straints (Rc) by enhancing handcrafted operators and model
architectures:
min D, s.t. R ≤ Rc. (1)
However, with the exponential growth of data volume and
the emergence of extremely bandwidth-constrained applica-
tions, such as satellite communications [
8, 9], the demand
for extreme-low bitrate compression has become increas-
ingly urgent. Traditional codecs and V AE-based methods
struggle to achieve such low bitrates (e.g., 0.01 bpp) with-
out incurring severe distortion.
Under such extreme bitrate constraints, the Rate-Distortion-
Perception (RDP) theory [
10] highlights that there is a bot-
tleneck in minimizing the distortion of the image, which
can be turned into maximizing the subjective quality of the
image. More speciﬁcally, minimizing distortion means re-
ducing the error between the reconstruction and the corre-
sponding input image, while maximizing perceptual qual-
ity means that the distribution of the reconstructed image is
close to the input image so that the user cannot tell which
is which. Recent advancements in generative models have
signiﬁcantly improved the ability to generate high-realistic
images [
11–18]. Given the ability of these models to cap-
ture intricate textures and enhance overall perceptual qual-
ity, recent image compression work combining Generative
Adversarial Networks (GANs) [
19–22] or Diffusion Mod-
els (DMs) [ 23–26] has achieved signiﬁcant optimization of
the perceptual quality of reconstructed images. By exploit-
ing the rich image modeling priors inherent in generative
models, these approaches allow for the reconstruction of
images with minimal artifacts using extremely few code-
words [
19, 27, 28].
To achieve the balance of Distortion-Perception (DP) under
bitrate constraints, existing work improves the ﬂexibility of
generative compression through ﬁxed-step reﬁnement [
24],
parameter-conditioned decoding [ 29, 30], and dual-branch
mixing [22], which is essentially to implicitly or explicitly
interpolate low-distortion and high-realism images. How-
ever, distortion can be measured not only on the whole im-
age but also within a single image while considering the
importance of different regions, which means reﬁning the
granularity of the distortion measure and thus decoupling it
from the constraints of perceptual quality. Intuitively, keep-
ing better consistency between the reconstruction and the
input image in the Regions of Interest (RoI) region while
relaxing the constraints in other regions by generating real-
istic content can obtain more satisfactory results. Especially
at extreme-low bitrates, the adaptive allocation of limited
bitrate resources (R) by generative compression is expected
to obtain better DP frontiers.
With this in mind, we propose the Map-guided-Masking
Realism Image Diffusion Codec (MRIDC) to achieve
endogenous-adaptive perceptual image compression at
extreme-low bitrate. In our framework, images are ﬁrst en-
coded into codebook indices through latent coding, where
the predeﬁned vector quantization space ensures a control-
lable extreme-low bitrate. By observing the correlation be-
tween the importance of regions in the original image and
the redundancy of elements in the latent space, we intro-
duce the Map-guided Latent Masking (MLM) module on
the coding side. The MLM module adaptively masks la-
tent representations based on the prior RoI information and
allocates limited bit resources to key areas, thereby main-
taining the consistency of details of the corresponding area
in the reconstructed image and the original image. On
the decoding side, we propose the Bidirectional Prediction
Controllable Generation (BPCG) module, which recon-
structs the masked indices by leveraging information from
neighboring elements. The explicit prediction completes
the latent information of the non-RoI region and smoothes
the coherence between it and the RoI region. The recon-
structed latent constrains the denoising process of the dif-
fusion model, resulting in a decoded image with low dis-
tortion of key details and optimal global perceptual qual-
ity. Experimental results demonstrate that MRIDC achieves
state-of-the-art perceptual compression quality at extremely
low bitrates, effectively preserving feature consistency in
key regions while allocating latent representation resources
18052

in a training-free manner. By leveraging the MLM and
BPCG modules, MRIDC enables conditional compression
based on various priors, ensuring higher consistency in re-
gions of interest (RoI) while delivering exceptional percep-
tual quality, as illustrated in Figure
raw_sections.method:
3. Method
MRIDC is designed to optimize the trade-off between lo-
cal distortion and global perceptual quality for extreme-
low bitrate image compression. Section
3.1 introduces the
task deﬁnition, followed by descriptions of the RoI-guided
Encoder and Diffusion-based Decoder in Sections
3.2
and 3.2.2. Finally, Section 3.3 details our training approach.
raw_sections.conclusion:
5. Conclusion
Considering the potential conﬂict between extremely low
bitrate compression and consistency of decoded image
details, we propose an adaptive compression framework
MRIDC based on the concept of RoI, which performs gen-
erative compression based on vector quantization space.
Given the RoI map, the MLM module designed at the en-
coder uses it to guide the distribution of bits in the latent
space in a training-free manner, thereby preserving more
details of the RoI. At the decoder, we introduce a BPCG
module for controllable generation, based on mask predic-
tion, to produce outputs with optimal perceptual quality.
Experimental results validate the effectiveness of the pro-
posed modules in maintaining image quality and demon-
strate the advancement of MRIDC in regional adaptive DP
balance. Given the current limitations on the size of the
latent space, we consider optimizing its ﬂexibility in the fu-
ture to support a wider range of variable bitrates.
